description: Outline of training examples for fine-tuning a small AINL model (~3B params). Total ~1200 examples; this file describes the buckets and provides representative samples.

buckets.name: core_basics
buckets.description: Basic statements, labels, jumps, conditionals, state/config declarations
sample_prompts: Write a program that counts from 1 to 10 and stores the final count in a state variable 'total'.
Declare a configuration threshold:N with default 5, and a state counter:N starting at 0.
Create two labels: L0 sets counter to 0 and jumps to L1; L1 increments counter 10 times then jumps to L2; L2 writes counter to cache.

buckets.name: core_arithmetic_string
buckets.description: Using core.add, sub, mul, div, concat, replace, parse, stringify
sample_prompts: Parse a JSON string from a variable 'input' and extract the 'price' field.
Concatenate three strings stored in variables a, b, c into one string separated by dashes.
Clamp a score between 0 and 100 using core.clamp.

positive_example_template.explanation: core.parse converts string to dict; bracket access

buckets.name: adapter_http
buckets.description: HTTP GET/POST with status checks, JSON handling, timeouts
sample_prompts: Fetch https://api.example.com/data and return the 'items' array from the JSON response.
POST to /webhook with JSON body {"event":"ping"} and check for 200 status.
Retry a GET request up to 3 times with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s) if status is 5xx.

positive_example_template.program:
R http.GET url ->resp
If (core.ne resp.status 200) ->error
X data (core.parse resp.body)['items']
positive_example_template.explanation: Check status before parsing; extract array

buckets.name: adapter_sqlite
buckets.description: SELECT queries, parameterized, safe table access
sample_prompts: Query all rows from table 'logs' where level = 'error' using a parameterized query.
Insert a new metrics row with fields ts (now), value into table 'metrics'.
Count active users today: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users WHERE last_active > ?

positive_example_template.program: R sqlite.query "SELECT * FROM logs WHERE level = ?" ["error"] ->rows

buckets.name: adapter_fs_cache
buckets.description: File read/write and cache get/set for persistence
sample_prompts: Read a JSON config file at startup and store it in a state variable 'config'.
Cache a value for 1 hour: set cache 'api' 'token' to a token string, expire in 3600 seconds.
Append a line to a log file using fs.read + fs.write (atomic append placeholder).

positive_example_template.explanation: Read and parse JSON into state

buckets.name: error_handling
buckets.description: Status checks, safe gets, try/except patterns, defaults
sample_prompts: Call an HTTP API and if status is not 200, log error and return default value [].
Read a cache key; if missing, compute and store default.
Wrap a db query in a safe pattern: on error, return empty list and write error to state.

positive_example_template.program:
R cache get "x" "y" ->val
If val ->found
X default 0
R cache set "x" "y" default
positive_example_template.explanation: Fallback to default when cache miss

buckets.name: openclaw_integration
buckets.description: Using email, calendar, social, db leads, svc, queue adapters
sample_prompts: Check unread emails and count how many are from 'boss@example.com'.
Get upcoming calendar events in the next 24 hours and format a summary string.
Check if services caddy and maddy are up; send alert if any are down.
Fetch all leads from the CRM and compute average rating.

positive_example_template.program:
R email G ->emails
X boss_count (len filter emails where (core.contains ? "boss@example.com"))
positive_example_template.explanation: email adapter returns list; filter by sender substring

buckets.name: full_programs
sample_prompts: Write a complete web server that serves /metrics and /.
Build a daily scraper that fetches a price and appends to CSV.
Implement a 5-minute monitor that checks services and throttles alerts to once per 15 minutes.

positive_example_template.explanation: Reference exact file; small variations are separate examples

format: JSONL with fields: {prompt, program, explanation, bucket, difficulty (easy|medium|hard), tags []}
generation_notes: Each positive example should be minimal (10–50 lines) and self-contained.
Negative examples should exhibit one common mistake (syntax, type, missing arg, wrong target) and include the corrected program.
For full programs, include the golden examples verbatim plus 20–30 small variants (different schedules, thresholds, or route paths).
Ensure adapter usage matches the ADAPTER_REGISTRY.json spec exactly.
All programs should compile with `compiler_v2.py` in strict_mode=false; run a subset through the runtime to verify.
